name: PubChem_get_associated_patents_by_CID
description: Get a list of patents associated with a specific compound CID.

cid.description: PubChem compound ID to query, e.g., 2244 (Aspirin).

fields.input_description: Input is a PubChem compound ID (integer).

name: PubChem_get_compound_properties_by_CID
description: Get a set of specified molecular properties through CID (Compound ID), such as molecular weight, IUPAC name, Canonical SMILES.

cid.description: PubChem compound ID to query, e.g., 2244 (Aspirin).

properties.description: Optional list of PubChem property names to retrieve (e.g. ['MolecularFormula','InChIKey','XLogP','IUPACName','MolecularWeight']). If omitted, returns a default set (MolecularWeight, IUPACName, SMILES). See PubChem PUG-REST property tables for valid names. Accepts a list or a comma-separated string.

fields.input_description: Input is a PubChem compound ID (integer).

name: PubChem_get_CID_by_compound_name
description: Search PubChem by compound name to get CID(s). This is the PRIMARY tool for looking up a drug or chemical by name (e.g., 'cytarabine', 'aspirin', 'metformin', 'venetoclax') to obtain PubChem Compound IDs. Use the returned CID with other PubChem tools for properties, patents, etc. Only works with chemical/drug names, not disease names.

name.description: Chemical compound name (e.g., "Aspirin", "Acetaminophen") or IUPAC name. Do not use disease names or medical conditions.

compound_name.description: Alias for name. The compound name to look up.

fields.input_description: Input is a chemical name string (case and space sensitive), PubChem will perform fuzzy matching automatically.

name: PubChem_get_CID_by_SMILES
description: Retrieve corresponding CID list by SMILES string.

fields.input_description: Input is a valid SMILES string (need to ensure beforehand that this SMILES has corresponding records in PubChem database).

name: PubChem_search_compounds_by_substructure
description: Search for CIDs in PubChem that contain the given substructure (SMILES). Returns up to max_results CIDs (default 10).

smiles.description: SMILES of substructure (e.g., "c1ccccc1" corresponds to benzene ring).

max_results.description: Maximum number of CIDs to return (default: 10, max: 10000).

fields.input_description: Input is SMILES of target substructure, PubChem will find all compounds containing this substructure. Returns up to max_results CIDs (default 10).

name: PubChem_search_compounds_by_similarity
description: Search by similarity (Tanimoto coefficient), returns CID list of compounds with similarity above threshold to given SMILES molecule. Returns up to max_results CIDs (default 10).

threshold.description: Similarity threshold (between 0 and 1), e.g., 0.9 means 90% similarity.

max_results.description: Maximum number of CIDs to return (default: 10, max: 10000).

name: PubChem_get_compound_2D_image_by_CID
description: Get 2D structure image (PNG format) of compound by CID.

cid.description: Compound ID to get image for, e.g., 2244.

fields.output_description: Returns binary PNG image data, conforming to PNG file header format. LLM can save this binary as image file, or provide to downstream visualization.

name: PubChem_get_compound_synonyms_by_CID
description: Get complete list of synonyms for compound by CID.

cid.description: Compound ID to query synonyms for, e.g., 2244.

fields.input_description: Input is compound ID (cid), PubChem will return all synonyms recorded in database for this compound.

name: PubChem_get_compound_xrefs_by_CID
description: Get external references (XRefs) for compound by CID, including links to ChEBI, DrugBank, KEGG, etc.

cid.description: Compound ID to query external references for, e.g., 2244.

xref_types.description: List of PubChem XRef types to query. Allowed values ONLY: RegistryID, RN, PubMedID, MMDBID, ProteinGI, NucleotideGI, TaxonomyID, MIMID, GeneID, ProbeID, PatentID, SourceName, SourceCategory, DBURL, SBURL. Database cross-references such as ChEBI, KEGG, ChEMBL and InChIKey are NOT xref types — they are listed inside the 'RegistryID' values, so use ["RegistryID"] to retrieve them.

name: PubChem_get_assay_description
description: Get bioassay description and metadata by AID. Returns assay name, source, protocol, target information, activity outcomes, and data columns. Essential for understanding what an assay measures before retrieving results.

name: PubChem_get_assay_summary
description: Get summary statistics for a bioassay by AID. Returns counts of active/inactive compounds, target information, and assay metadata. Useful for quick assessment of assay size and hit rate.

name: PubChem_get_assay_targets
description: Get protein/gene targets for a bioassay. Returns GI numbers, protein names, gene IDs, and gene symbols. Critical for understanding what molecular targets an assay screens against.

name: PubChem_search_assays_by_target_gene
description: Search bioassays by target gene symbol. Returns AIDs of assays that screen against the specified gene. Example: USP2 returns assays targeting USP2 protease.

gene_symbol.description: Gene symbol to search (e.g., EGFR, USP2, TP53)

name: PubChem_search_assays_by_type
description: Search bioassays by assay type. Types: screening (HTS), confirmatory, doseresponse, summary, cellbased, biochemical, invivo, invitro. Returns list of matching AIDs.

assay_type.description: Type of assay to search for

name: PubChem_get_compound_bioactivity
description: Get bioassay results summary for a compound by CID. Returns all assays the compound was tested in, activity outcomes (active/inactive), and target information. Critical for understanding compound's biological profile.

name: PubChem_get_assay_active_compounds
description: Get CIDs of compounds that are ACTIVE in a bioassay. Returns compound IDs that passed the activity threshold. Use for hit identification from HTS screens.

name: PubChem_get_assay_dose_response
description: Get dose-response data from a bioassay. Returns concentration-response curves for tested compounds. Essential for determining IC50/EC50 values. Limited to dose-response type assays.

aid.description: PubChem BioAssay ID (must be a dose-response assay)

name: PubChem_get_assays_for_compound_active
description: Get AIDs of assays where a compound is ACTIVE. Returns list of bioassay IDs where the compound showed activity. Use to understand a compound's target profile.

name: PubChem_get_substance_by_SID
description: Retrieve a PubChem SUBSTANCE (SID, depositor-level) record by its Substance ID. Unlike the CID/compound tools, this returns the raw depositor submission: the source/depositor name, the depositor's own source identifier, deposited synonyms, comments (often SMILES/InChI), cross-references (registry IDs, patents), and the list of linked compound CIDs that PubChem standardized this substance into. Returns found=false when the SID does not exist.

sid.description: PubChem Substance ID (SID), a positive integer, e.g., 223766453.

fields.input_description: Input is a PubChem Substance ID (sid). The tool fetches the raw PC_Substances record plus the linked compound CIDs.
fields.output_description: Returns {status, found, data, metadata}. On found=true, data has: sid, source_name (depositor source id), source_id (depositor's own record id), synonyms (list), comment (list, often SMILES/InChI), registry_ids (list), xrefs (raw list), linked_cids (list of CIDs). On a non-existent SID, found=false.

name: PubChem_get_substances_by_source
description: Reverse sourcing: list ALL PubChem substance SIDs deposited/offered by a specific chemical vendor or data source (e.g., 'Combi-Blocks', 'Sigma-Aldrich', 'Enamine', 'TCI'). This is the inverse of the forward CID->vendor lookup (PubChem_get_compound_xrefs_by_CID): it answers 'what does vendor X actually stock/sell' by returning that depositor's full substance (SID) catalog. The 'source' name must match a PubChem registered substance source name exactly (case-sensitive) -- use PubChem_list_substance_sources first to validate/normalize the vendor name. Result lists can be very large (hundreds of thousands of SIDs).

source.description: Exact PubChem substance source / vendor name (case-sensitive), e.g., "Combi-Blocks", "Sigma-Aldrich", "Enamine". Multi-word names with spaces are accepted (e.g., "Aaron Chemicals LLC"). Validate the name with PubChem_list_substance_sources to avoid an empty/404 result.

vendor.description: Alias for source. The vendor/source name to retrieve SIDs for.

fields.input_description: Input is a single 'source' (vendor/depositor) name exactly as registered in PubChem. The tool requests every substance SID attributed to that source.
fields.output_description: Returns {status:'success', data:{IdentifierList:{SID:[...]}}}. data.IdentifierList.SID is the list of all PubChem Substance IDs offered/deposited by that source. Use each SID with PubChem_get_substance_by_SID to expand to the depositor record and linked compound CIDs. An unknown/misspelled source name yields a PubChem HTTP 404 returned as status:'error'.

name: PubChem_list_substance_sources
description: Vendor/source directory: enumerate the full master roster of chemical vendors and data sources registered as PubChem SUBSTANCE depositors (~919 sources, including Enamine, Sigma-Aldrich, TCI, Combi-Blocks). Use this to discover which suppliers PubChem aggregates and to validate/normalize a vendor name (exact, case-sensitive) BEFORE a reverse-sourcing query with PubChem_get_substances_by_source. Takes no parameters.

fields.input_description: No input parameters. Returns the complete substance source directory.
fields.output_description: Returns {status:'success', data:{InformationList:{SourceName:[...]}}}. data.InformationList.SourceName is the alphabetical list of every registered substance source/vendor name. Each name can be used verbatim as the 'source' argument of PubChem_get_substances_by_source.
